name: Clockify Time Tracking API
what_it_does: Free time tracking platform with comprehensive REST API — managing time entries, projects, clients, tasks, and reports for teams and freelancers, with unlimited users on free plan and API for automation and integration with billing tools.
use_cases: Logging time entries programmatically from external project tools via REST API
Querying time reports for projects and clients for invoicing automation
Syncing Clockify time data to accounting tools (Xero, QuickBooks) via API
Managing projects, clients, and tasks programmatically for team onboarding
Building time-tracking integrations with development tools (Jira, GitHub) via API
not_for: Complex resource planning with capacity forecasting (use Float or Runn)
GPS-based field workforce tracking (use Hubstaff or TSheets)
Enterprise HR time and attendance with payroll integration
best_when: A team needs free unlimited time tracking with a comprehensive API for automation, reporting, and integration with billing and project tools.
avoid_when: You need resource planning, scheduling, or GPS tracking features — Clockify focuses on time logging.

auth.notes: API key from user profile settings. X-Api-Key header. User-scoped access. Workspace ID required in most API paths. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Single API key per user.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited users, unlimited projects, unlimited time tracking
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard from $5.49/user/month; Pro from $7.99/user/month; Enterprise from $11.99/user/month
pricing.notes: Very generous free tier — unlimited users and projects. Paid plans add advanced features (invoicing, expenses, scheduling). Serbian company (Novi Sad). 5M+ users. One of the most widely-used free time tracking tools. API available on all plans.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits: 10 requests/second per API key

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Comprehensive REST API covering time entries, projects, clients, tasks, users, and reports. Good documentation with clear examples. Workspace ID in URL path is consistent. Webhook support for time entry events. Rate limits well-documented (10 req/s).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Time entry creation creates new entries — not idempotent. Project/client creation idempotent by name within workspace. Time entry reads always idempotent. Updates idempotent for same values.
known_agent_gotchas: Workspace ID required in every API path — fetch from /workspaces endpoint first
API key is user-level — each team member has their own key for their data
Report API returns aggregated data — raw time entries require time entries API
Rate limit of 10 req/s — batch operations need throttling logic

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant — Serbian/EU company. EU and US data residency. API key auth. No OAuth2 scopes. User-level key prevents cross-user data access by design. No formal certifications beyond GDPR.
